rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromide-mediated sulfoximido-selenylation of unsaturated sulfoximines with diselenides to give cyclic sulfoximidoyl heterocycles bearing -SeR groups
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br3+ which then oxidizes PhSeSePh to a selenium radical/radical cation; bromide acts as redox mediator

key_intermediates.name: PhSe radical and/or PhSe cation from anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh
Carbon-centered radical/cation adduct I from PhSe addition to alkene of unsaturated sulfoximine
Further oxidized intermediate II (carbocation) or aziridinium-type III after deprotonation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br3+, which oxidizes PhSeSePh to PhSe radical/cation; this adds to the alkene of the unsaturated sulfoximine generating intermediate I, which is further oxidized to a cation (II) and then trapped by intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the sulfoximine N followed by deprotonation to give the cyclic product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still formed in 35% yield; only partial suppression, suggesting radical pathway may not be dominant

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a formed in 67% yield; little suppression observed

probe_reagents.name: nBu4NBr (cyclic voltammetry)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower oxidation peak and obvious catalytic current observed, confirming Br- acts as redox mediator

notes: Authors propose two pathways in Scheme 4: (A) anodic oxidation pathway via Br3+ mediator generating PhSe radical/cation, favored and discussed first; (B) alternative cathodic reduction of PhSeSePh to give PhSe- + PhSe radical, supported by prior literature. The incomplete suppression by TEMPO/BHT led authors to suggest a radical process may not be the main pathway, implying the cationic (path a via radical cation) route may dominate.
